name: Corry Bliss
body:
| Peter -- We did it! Today, we blew past our Super Saturday goal of contacting 50,000 voters in a single day – we contacted 67,544 Ohioans!

Rob and Jane started the day in Brecksville and made their way across the state with stops in Toledo, Dayton, Columbus, and Cincinnati. Across Ohio, more than 800 volunteers and interns helped us meet and exceed our goal.

**[](http://www.robportman.com/super_saturday_april?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=ssapril5&n=1)**
We’re focused on building the most organized grassroots team that Ohio has ever seen. We’ve now contacted nearly 1.7 million voters since May of last year – and we’re just getting started! On the other hand, Ted Strickland’s low energy campaign is hiding from his record. Today alone, Rob made more public appearances than Ted did in the previous month! National Democrats have said that the path to winning the White House and the Senate majority runs through Ohio. That’s why they’ve already spent more than $2 million attacking Rob on TV across Ohio—and Ted’s liberal allies just reserved $10 million in attack ads for the fall!

[Contribute now to help make sure we have the resources to fight back](https://robportman.nationbuilder.com/donate?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=ssapril5&n=2)!

We appreciate your support – thank you for standing with Rob! Sincerely, Corry Bliss[

name: Joe Sestak
subject: Watch: The Final Debate of the Primary
body:
[](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/171984d/3476a20e/dae4557/27424a21/1027121531/VEsH/ "http://joesestak.com")
---

Peter, I have spent this campaign focused on the issues. I kicked off the campaign by walking 422 miles across the Commonwealth, doing an event each day on a different policy important to the people of the towns through which I was walking. Then, I released a 250-page policy book so the people would know my plan for restoring the American Dream and the policies I will pursue in the Senate to put the Dream back within reach, and I then toured the state to talk about each chapter of the book. As other candidates entered the race, I then put out my Plan for PA Working Families, putting into about 50 pages the policies most important to the people I seek to represent. I also released a companion Plan for Securing America in the 21st Century, addressing the spectrum of national security threats we face in keeping America and the world secure, so that we all might prosper. It's because of these policy events that PA's leading pollster and political analyst Terry Madonna said yesterday, **" More and more I'm hearing most experts say that Sestak, the ****indefatigable****campaigner, can pull it out. "** At 11 AM, the final Democratic primary debate will air on 6ABC in Philadelphia. I would be honored if you would watch this final debate this morning, and [make another donation during the debate so that I can bring forward my policy platforms to the general election against Pat Toomey.](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/171984d/3476a20e/dae4557/27424a20/1027121531/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gotyour6?refcode=16EM04241FBp1") No other Senate candidate - across the country - has committed to having an actual policy discussion like I have. Please help me continue that discussion by contributing today, and at 11 AM you can click the link below the contribute button to access the live stream of the debate online! | [](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/171984d/3476a20e/dae4557/27424a2f/1027121531/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gotyour6?refcode=16EM04241FBb1")

name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: Donald Trump is trying to trick you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a campaign supported by more than one million grassroots donors
body:
We have to hold Trump accountable | Friend --

Donald Trump is preparing to pivot from this Republican primary to the general election -- and in order to make himself palatable to voters outside the Republican base, he’s preparing to undergo an extreme image makeover.

“I’m very capable of changing to anything I want to change to,” he’s said. “At the right time, I will be so presidential, you will be so bored.”

Trump thinks he can bully us, fool us, or cajole us into forgetting how he has derided women, Mexican Americans, Muslims, immigrants, and people with disabilities. He thinks he can trick the country into making him our president.

But we know the truth of who Donald Trump is, and we’re not going to let anyone forget it.

We’re not going to forget that Donald Trump called Mexican Americans criminals and rapists on the day he launched his campaign.

Or that he called for a ban on Muslims coming to America -- and surveillance of Muslims and mosques on American soil.

We won’t forget that he said he’d eliminate gun-free zones in schools on his first day in office, or that he advocated for the murder of innocent family members of suspected terrorists -- that’s a war crime, by the way.

We’ll remember how he mocked a disabled journalist with crude gestures, and we’ll remember how he implied a female journalist who asked him tough questions was menstruating.

We won’t forget that he said women who get abortions deserve “some form of punishment.”

We’ll remember how he incited violence at his rallies, encouraging his supporters to “knock the crap out of” protesters.

And we won’t forget that when he received the endorsement of the notorious former leader of the Ku Klux Klan, Trump said he didn’t know enough about white supremacists to disavow their support.

The prospect of a Trump presidency is terrifying -- and if we don’t hold him accountable for what he has said and done in this primary, he could win.

So here’s what we need to do: Get to know his real record backwards and forwards. Talk to our friends and neighbors to make sure they know it, too. Grow a nationwide community that refuses to let Trump get away with what he’s said and done in this election -- we won’t let him fool voters into thinking he’s any kind of reasonable.

clean_body: We have to hold Trump accountable | Friend -- Donald Trump is preparing to pivot from this Republican primary to the general election -- and in order to make himself palatable to voters outside the Republican base, he’s preparing to undergo an extreme image makeover. “I’m very capable of changing to anything I want to change to,” he’s said. “At the right time, I will be so presidential, you will be so bored.” Trump thinks he can bully us, fool us, or cajole us into forgetting how he has derided women, Mexican Americans, Muslims, immigrants, and people with disabilities. He thinks he can trick the country into making him our president. But we know the truth of who Donald Trump is, and we’re not going to let anyone forget it. We’re not going to forget that Donald Trump called Mexican Americans criminals and rapists on the day he launched his campaign. Or that he called for a ban on Muslims coming to America -- and surveillance of Muslims and mosques on American soil. We won’t forget that he said he’d eliminate gun-free zones in schools on his first day in office, or that he advocated for the murder of innocent family members of suspected terrorists -- that’s a war crime, by the way. We’ll remember how he mocked a disabled journalist with crude gestures, and we’ll remember how he implied a female journalist who asked him tough questions was menstruating. We won’t forget that he said women who get abortions deserve “some form of punishment.” We’ll remember how he incited violence at his rallies, encouraging his supporters to “knock the crap out of” protesters. And we won’t forget that when he received the endorsement of the notorious former leader of the Ku Klux Klan, Trump said he didn’t know enough about white supremacists to disavow their support. The prospect of a Trump presidency is terrifying -- and if we don’t hold him accountable for what he has said and done in this primary, he could win. So here’s what we need to do: Get to know his real record backwards and forwards. Talk to our friends and neighbors to make sure they know it, too. Grow a nationwide community that refuses to let Trump get away with what he’s said and done in this election -- we won’t let him fool voters into thinking he’s any kind of reasonable. name: Joe Sestak
subject: Sunday in America
body:
[](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/1719857/3476a20e/daee85b/274248c2/3792172775/VEsH/ "http://joesestak.com")
---

Peter, Starting at 6:30 am this morning, I attended seven congregations, and even spoke at each... as I've done most weekends the past few years. Today was African American churches, but I visit mosques to temples, just as I did as a Congressman. I not only try to meet people at events, but to visit them where their lives are, to better understand and admire our American character.

| [](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/1719857/3476a20e/daee85b/274248c1/3792172775/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gotyour6?refcode=16EM04242FBb1")
---

It is one of the reasons that when I left Congress I spurned the lobbying offers and went to teach at Cheyney University - our nation's oldest Historically Black University - and later hired for this campaign a young man I taught there who was trying to make a go again after being incarcerated for drugs.
And it is why I ask for your support, so I can continue to practice what one black pastor preached to me, "Congressman, I'll know you are good when what you do speaks so loudly, I cannot hear what you say.
[](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/1719857/3476a20e/daee85b/274248c0/3792172775/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gotyour6?refcode=16EM04242FBb2")
---
That is the type of public servant I want to continue to be in the Senate - measured by deeds not words - and [I ask for your support in helping me bring an integrity of service back to public service. Thank you.](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/1719857/3476a20e/daee85b/274248cf/3792172775/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gotyour6?refcode=16EM04242FBp1")
Warmly,
Joe

**The Florida seat of Senator Marco Rubio is universally considered a toss-up**
_\-- NY Mag, 4/21/16_
It's been a big week for our race, everyone:
* First, _USA Today_ revealed that **our race is the one that will determine Senate control.**
* Then, _NY Mag_ confirmed **Florida is "universally considered a toss-up."**
* Now, the GOP is taking notice: they'll "make Florida a priority" and "swing into action."

A true toss-up that will determine Senate control? We don't have a moment to lose.

So we set an emergency goal for tonight so we can be prepared for the onslaught of Republican attacks that are headed our way. We still have $2,567 left to raise, and we might not make it. **Will you pitch in to help us meet our goal, retake Rubio 's seat, and save our chances for a Democratic Senate?**
